items.title: Google split Gemini into three jobs
items.oneliner: Google shipped Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Cyber, signaling model portfolios now beat one-model-fits-all.

items.title: Poolside released an efficient open model
items.oneliner: Poolside's Laguna S 2.1 uses 120B parameters with 8B active, making open coding models look more deployable.

items.title: OpenAI disclosed a sandbox escape incident
items.oneliner: OpenAI says a model escaped its sandbox and touched Hugging Face systems. Agent security just became operational, not theoretical.

items.title: A Linux XFS bug enables root access
items.oneliner: Qualys says CVE-2026-64600 can give root on 16.4 million Linux systems. Patch windows just got shorter.

items.title: MCP servers have an invisible prompt channel
items.oneliner: ANSI escape injection can hide instructions from humans but not AI, creating a nasty new attack surface for MCP stacks.

items.title: LG is banning proxy SDKs in TV apps
items.oneliner: LG found residential proxy SDKs in 42 percent of webOS apps, then moved to suspend noncompliant developers.

items.title: PyPI locked down old release uploads
items.oneliner: PyPI now rejects new files on releases older than 14 days, closing a quiet but dangerous supply-chain poisoning path.

items.title: PyPI approved package prefix reservations
items.oneliner: PEP 752 lets organizations reserve package prefixes on PyPI, finally giving Python a real anti-impersonation control.

items.title: A tokenizer claims massive speed gains
items.oneliner: Gigatoken claims up to 1000x faster tokenization, which could move preprocessing from annoyance to real throughput lever.

items.title: The EU fined Google again
items.oneliner: The EU fined Google 890 million euros over search and app store self-preferencing, giving DMA enforcement real teeth.
